description: Fix tool-http-api.e2e.ts:81 E2E failure on macOS CI. The 'plugin filter returns only matching tools' test fails. This is blocking CI from being fully green.

userStories.title: Fix tool-http-api plugin filter E2E test failing on macOS
userStories.description: The test at tool-http-api.e2e.ts:81 fails on macOS CI. Investigate the root cause — whether it's a timing issue, a platform-specific behavior difference, or a real bug in the HTTP API plugin filter — and fix it.
acceptanceCriteria: All other tool-http-api tests continue to pass
The fix works on all 3 platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)
npx playwright test e2e/tool-http-api.e2e.ts passes with --retries=2
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/tool-http-api.e2e.ts (the failing test at line 81)
- platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts (HTTP API implementation)

Context:
The test 'plugin filter returns only matching tools' at line 81 fails on macOS CI but passes on Linux. The failure is in the macOS E2E step of the CI workflow.

Investigation approach:
1. Read the test at line 81 to understand what it asserts
2. Check if it depends on plugin discovery order or timing that differs on macOS
3. Check if the HTTP API's plugin filter has a case-sensitivity issue (macOS filesystem is case-insensitive)
4. Run the test locally and check the actual vs expected response
5. If it's a timing issue (plugin not yet discovered when the filter query runs), add proper waitForHealth or polling
6. If it's a real bug in the filter logic, fix the HTTP route handler

Constraints:
- Do NOT skip the test — fix the underlying issue
- The fix must work on all platforms
- CI must be fully green after this fix
